articles.title: Who Might Host the Morning Radio Show?
articles.summary: Scott Mills was the host of a popular morning radio show on BBC Radio 2, but he had to leave his job. Now, the radio station needs to find a new person to host the show. Two main people are being talked about as possible new hosts. Vernon Kay is a favorite because he already hosts another big show on Radio 2 with many listeners. Sara Cox is another choice because she has experience hosting morning shows before and works on Radio 2 too. The station has to think carefully because changing hosts can affect other shows. This is a big decision for the radio station to make.

articles.title: Kanye West is Coming Back to a Music Festival in London
articles.summary: Kanye West, a famous rapper from the US, will be the main performer at the Wireless Festival in London this year. He will play songs from his albums over three nights in July. This is his first big show in the UK since 2015. Kanye has said sorry for making hurtful comments about Jewish people in the past. The festival organizers are excited for his return. He recently released a new album called Bully. No other performers for the festival have been announced yet.

articles.title: Drivers Hope for Money Back from Car Loans
articles.summary: Many drivers in the UK hope to get money back because they were sold car loans unfairly. A group called the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) says about 40% of people who got car loans between 2007 and 2024 might get compensation. Poppy Whiteside from Kent is one driver who had to send many letters to her car finance company to ask for her money. She bought a Ford Fiesta in 2018 and later found out she was not told about a special commission deal that made her pay more interest. The FCA banned these deals in 2021 because they can make people pay too much. Now, drivers who were not told about such deals can claim money back. The FCA thinks 12 million people could get about £829 each. Some banks' share prices went up because fewer people than expected might get compensation.

articles.title: Eurovision Song Contest Starts in Asia for the First Time
articles.summary: The Eurovision Song Contest, a big music competition from Europe, is now coming to Asia for the first time. Ten countries in Asia, like South Korea, Thailand, and the Philippines, will join. They will have their own shows to pick songs, and then a big final will be in Bangkok on November 14. Eurovision started in 1956 and is the world's longest-running music contest. Martin Green, a director, said it's special to include Asia because it has lots of culture and talent. People on social media are excited, with some saying the Philippines will win. This is not the first time Eurovision has tried to grow; it had an American version in 2022, but not many people watched. Organizers hope Asia will do better because people there love music, like K-pop from South Korea. Other countries have their own music styles too.

articles.title: How a War Affects Houses and Money
articles.summary: A bank called Nationwide says that the UK housing market might slow down because of a war in Iran. This war is making energy prices go up, which means people have to pay more for things like heating and electricity. It also makes mortgage rates higher, so it costs more to borrow money to buy a house. In March, house prices went up a little, but the bank warns that if the war lasts a long time, it could hurt the housing market. Experts say that first-time buyers might find it harder to buy a home, and families might need to spend less money. However, many people have saved money in recent years, which could help them manage these costs. The bank is watching the situation closely to see what happens next.

articles.title: A New Idea About How the Universe Started
articles.summary: Scientists have a new idea about how the Big Bang happened. They think the universe's fast early growth came from a theory called quantum gravity, which mixes gravity with tiny particles. This idea is simpler because it doesn't need extra parts like other explanations. It also predicts tiny ripples in space called gravitational waves that we might detect with experiments. The scientists, led by Dr. Niayesh Afshordi, were surprised their idea can be tested with real data. This could help us understand the universe's beginning better as new tools make measurements more accurate.
